title: Ending Foreign Site Reference Number process and foreign warehouses - Canada.ca
content:
## Ending Foreign Site Reference Number process and foreign warehouses
The Natural Health Products Site Licensing and Good Manufacturing Practices Inspection Bulletin No. 10, February 16, 2026, from the Health Product Compliance Directorate
- Why the FSRN process is ending
- Foreign warehouses no longer required to be listed on site licences
The objectives of this bulletin are to:
- inform applicants that the Foreign Site Reference Number (FSRN) process is ending and explain why
- explain how this service is now integrated into the site licencing program
- inform applicants that they do not need to apply to add foreign warehouses to a site licence
An FSRN is a unique reference number issued by Health Canada to sites located outside of Canada (foreign sites) that manufacture, package or label Natural Health Products (NHP). An FSRN indicates that a foreign site has undergone a partial assessment and was found compliant with certain sections of Part 3 Good Manufacturing Practices (GMP) of the Natural Health Products Regulations .
- is not required under the Regulations
- is not equivalent to a site licence for a foreign site
- does not authorize a foreign site to export NHPs directly into Canada without a licenced Canadian importer

## Why the FSRN process is ending
Health Canada is committed to improving efficiency while continuing to deliver high quality services that protect the health and safety of Canadians. As part of ongoing efforts to streamline our processes and reduce burden, we reviewed our NHP site licence assessment processes and determined that the FSRN process is no longer necessary.
Ending the FSRN process will benefit both the industry and Health Canada by:
- aligning the review of foreign site GMP evidence with the site licence process
- simplifying and consolidating the submission process by eliminating the need for FSRN applications, reducing confusion
- focussing our efforts on regulated site licence submissions and preventing site licensing delays
Direct communication between Health Canada and foreign sites will continue to be available, as needed, to maintain confidentiality of the submitted information.
Foreign sites that previously relied on the FSRN process to be added to an importer's site licence must now submit the same GMP evidence through the site licensing pathway. Applicants should refer to the Site Licensing Guidance Document for options on acceptable GMP evidence.
Although the Harmonized Site Licence and FSRN Application Form and the Site Licensing Guidance Document still reference the FSRN authorization as acceptable GMP evidence, this information is outdated and will be removed from Health Canada's website in the near future.
Existing FSRN holders will be listed as 'discontinued' in the Site Licence and Foreign Site Reference Number Holders List . Any new FSRN application will be rejected.
Foreign sites may continue to communicate directly with Health Canada, as needed, using the site licence generic email ( hpcd.nhp.sl-dcps.psn.le@hc-sc.gc.ca ).

## Foreign warehouses no longer required to be listed on site licences
Site licence holders are no longer required to submit applications to add warehouses located outside of Canada to their site licence.
Health Canada will no longer list foreign warehouses on site licences. Going forward, previously approved foreign warehouses will be removed from site licences when the licence is updated, such as through an amendment or renewal.
Although foreign warehouses are no longer required to be listed on the site licence, they must still meet all applicable GMP requirements of the regulations .
These changes come into effect on February 16, 2026. After this date, we will no longer accept FSRN applications and requests to add warehouses located outside of Canada to a site licence.
For questions, please contact: hpcd.nhp.sl-dcps.psn.le@hc-sc.gc.ca .
